title: JSON string literals should be well-formed
messageFormat: This JSON literal is malformed and will fail to parse at runtime.
summary: When a constant string is handed to a JSON parser, malformed content is only discovered when the line runs and throws. A missing brace, a trailing comma, or an unquoted key turns into a runtime parse failure that a quick check of the literal would have caught.
Fix the literal so it is valid JSON before it reaches the parser.

seeAlso.text: RFC 8259: The JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) Data Interchange Format
